summary: High‑energy early ’68 set with blistering Viola Lee Blues & soaring Clementine
blurb: The show erupts with a ferocious Viola Lee Blues opening, then moves into a jazzy, high‑octane Clementine and a tight The Other One sequence. Jerry’s guitar soars throughout, while the rhythm section locks in on every groove. It’s a compact, raw performance that captures the Dead at peak early‑68 intensity.
review: Portland’s Crystal Ballroom on February 2, 1968 delivered one of the band’s most electrifying early‑68 outings. From the opening howl of “Viola Lee Blues,” the quintet rockets into an almost psychedelic stretch of “Cryptical Envelopment” → “The Other One” before slipping back into a jazzy, soaring rendition of “Clementine.” Jerry Garcia’s tone is described as “insane licks” and “wailing,” especially on the blues‑driven opener and the melodic Clementine, while Phil Lesh provides rock‑solid, time‑keeping bass that grounds the high‑energy flow. The set closes with a gritty “Good Morning Little School Girl,” rounding out a concise yet powerful half‑hour of music that feels both raw and tightly focused. Across three soundboard sources the performance is uniformly praised, making this show a quintessential snapshot of the Dead’s early psychedelic fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, consistently praising Jerry’s soaring guitar, the explosive Viola Lee Blues, jazzy Clementine and tight The Other One sequence. No negative comments appear about performance quality, indicating strong positive sentiment across sources.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Charlie‑Miller soundboard transfer with crystal‑clear mix; captures the full set without cuts and highlights the band’s high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Explosive Viola Lee Blues opening that sets a primal tone
Jazzy, soaring Clementine showcasing Garcia’s wailing guitar
Tight, psychedelic The Other One sequence following Cryptical Envelopment
Overall raw, high‑energy early‑68 vibe captured in three solid SBD sources

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar soars with insane licks and wailing tone, especially on Viola Lee Blues and Clementine; praised as a highlight across all recordings.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides solid, time‑keeping bass that anchors the high‑energy flow, noted for tightness on Clementine.
